summary: Large mndatadel decomp/matching PR that replaced multiple placeholder functions with C implementations, added mndatadel-specific structs/static model descriptors, adjusted nearby menu files mostly for include/order/formatting, and produced bot-reported progress including a full match for mnDataDel_8024E940 plus substantial partial improvements for other mndatadel functions.

key_files.role: Primary decomp work: added includes and implemented mnDataDel_8024E940, mnDataDel_8024EA6C, fn_8024F318, fn_8024F840, fn_8024FD40, mnDataDel_8024FE4C, and mnDataDel_80250170; adjusted fn_8024ECCC to use MnDataDelData access.
Header/static layout support: changed MnDataDelData contiguous fields x3C..x50 into x3C[6], added MnDataDelGObjUserData, and added/static-declared mndatadel data/model descriptors.
Adjacent menu formatting/include-order cleanup; no substantive behavior change is evident from the loaded diff excerpt.
Adjacent menu formatting cleanup for line wrapping and expression formatting; no substantive behavior change is evident from the loaded diff excerpt.

what_changed.text: Implemented previously placeholder mndatadel functions, with the bot reporting mnDataDel_8024E940 as a new 100% match and several other functions as partial improvements.
Added includes needed by the new mndatadel code, including gobjuserdata, memory, gm/gmmain headers, lbarchive, lbaudio_ax, lbcardgame, debug, and toy.
Converted six consecutive MnDataDelData scalar fields x3C through x50 into s32 x3C[6], enabling indexed access in loops and cursor handling.
Added struct MnDataDelGObjUserData with byte state fields, an HSD_Text pointer, and HSD_JObj* x10[8], then used it for gobj user_data allocation and access.
Used HSD_ASSERTREPORT around HSD_MemAlloc user_data allocation in mnDataDel_8024FE4C.
Bot report recorded +300 bytes matched code, +48 bytes matched data, 2 new matches, and 9 unmatched-item improvements for main/melee/mn/mndatadel.

smart_moves.text: Recovering x3C as an array allowed loops over six menu cursor/option entries instead of leaving six unrelated scalar fields.
Introducing MnDataDelGObjUserData made repeated gobj user_data accesses reviewable enough to distinguish state bytes, text pointer, and JObj child slots.
The implementation keeps asset/resource strings inline in lbArchive_LoadSections rather than moving them to named string symbols.
Natural loops were used for repeated JObj setup and animation updates where indexed data was available.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When menu data has consecutive fields that are consumed by indexed loops or cursor movement, consider whether the original layout was an array before preserving generated scalar-field access.
For gobj user_data-heavy menu code, adding a local struct can expose state bytes, text handles, and JObj arrays enough to replace some raw access patterns, even if field names remain provisional.
A single focused menu decomp PR may include nearby formatting-only churn, but evidence for decomp progress should be tied to the touched object/function report, not inferred from formatting edits.

naming_conventions.name: MnDataDelGObjUserData
naming_conventions.observation: Semantic type name was added for mndatadel gobj user data, while individual fields remained offset-style x0/x1/x10 because stronger semantics are not evident in the slice.

naming_conventions.name: mnDataDel_8024E940_inline
naming_conventions.observation: A narrow inline wrapper around mn_8022F298 was introduced and used in mnDataDel_8024E940; the slice does not explain why this inline was required.

naming_conventions.name: x3C[6]
naming_conventions.observation: Consecutive address-style fields were represented as an array while preserving the offset-style base name.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK was added in mnDataDel_8024EA6C, fn_8024ECCC, and fn_8024FD40.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The loaded evidence shows PAD_STACK use but does not include an objdiff explanation; keep this as PR-specific tactic evidence, not a global style rule.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used a do-while loop with pointer increment over mnDataDel_803EF870.x3C for six menu entries.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Supported by changed header layout, but exact compiler-shape reason is not documented in comments.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used switch dispatch for cursor cases in fn_8024F318, with repeated JObj animation restore blocks in several cases.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Repeated blocks may invite helper/loop exploration, but this PR evidence does not prove a cleaner abstraction would match.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Used comma expressions/casts in a few calls, such as HSD_JObjReqAnimAll(sp18, (0, 1.0f)) and HSD_JObjAnimAll((0, sp18)).
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as local matching artifact unless later evidence explains it.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Kept inline asset names in lbArchive_LoadSections while adding static model descriptors.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Consistent with loaded standards on string literals, but the PR body does not discuss data ownership.

review_feedback.author: decomp-dev[bot]
review_feedback.text: GALE01 report: matched code 66.41% (+0.01%, +300 bytes), matched data 38.72% (+0.00%, +48 bytes); 2 new matches including mnDataDel_8024E940 and .bss; 9 improvements including fn_8024F318, mnDataDel_8024FE4C, fn_8024F840, .data, mnDataDel_8024EA6C, mnDataDel_80250170, fn_8024FD40, fn_8024ECCC, and .sdata2.
No human review comments were present in the loaded PR slice.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs touching MnDataDelGObjUserData field names x0 x1 x2 x3 x10
Search for cleanup of PAD_STACK in mnDataDel_8024EA6C fn_8024ECCC fn_8024FD40
Search mndatadel.c for repeated HSD_JObjReqAnimAll mn_8022F3D8 HSD_JObjAnimAll animation restore pattern
Find later changes to mnDataDel_803EF870 x3C array or MnDataDelData field naming
Search for mnDataDel_8024E940_inline removal or explanation
Find PRs improving fn_8024F318 fn_8024F840 mnDataDel_8024FE4C matching percentages

notes: PR body is empty and there are no human review comments in the loaded slice.
The raw diff loaded in the prompt was truncated, but targeted local slice lookup provided the static header and adjacent file diff portions needed for classification.
Bot report gives progress percentages and matched/improved item names, but no objdiff command transcript or author rationale is included.
Some tactics such as PAD_STACK, comma expressions, and an inline wrapper are visible in the diff but not explained by reviewer or author comments.

accepted_candidate_records.title: PR 2448 mndatadel matching progress
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR 2448 implemented several mndatadel functions and bot-reported a 100% match for mnDataDel_8024E940 plus partial improvements for fn_8024F318, mnDataDel_8024FE4C, fn_8024F840, mnDataDel_8024EA6C, mnDataDel_80250170, fn_8024FD40, and fn_8024ECCC.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Recover arrays from consecutive menu data fields when indexed use appears
accepted_candidate_records.text: In mndatadel, six consecutive scalar fields x3C..x50 were changed to x3C[6] and then used by loops/cursor indexing, making the source align with repeated menu-entry logic.

source_update_candidates.title: mndatadel static layout facts from PR 2448
source_update_candidates.text: PR 2448 changed struct MnDataDelData to use s32 x3C[6] in place of six consecutive scalar fields and added struct MnDataDelGObjUserData containing u8 state fields x0-x8, HSD_Text* xC, and HSD_JObj* x10[8].
source_update_candidates.reason: May help future agents classify mndatadel user_data and array-indexed menu data without rediscovering the PR.

source_update_candidates.title: Treat unexplained PAD_STACK and comma-expression call arguments as local tactics requiring evidence
source_update_candidates.text: This PR visibly used PAD_STACK and comma-expression call arguments while producing matching progress, but the slice contains no author/reviewer explanation. If promoted, phrase narrowly as a reminder to record objdiff evidence for such tactics rather than as approval of the tactic.
source_update_candidates.reason: Could reinforce existing matching-tactics standards with a concrete historical example, but evidence is only diff-level.

rejection_notes: Do not infer that all functions in the PR fully matched; the bot report shows only mnDataDel_8024E940 as a function-level 100% new match, while many others are partial improvements.
Do not promote the mndatadel field names x0/x1/x3/x10 as semantic names; the PR preserves offset-style names and the slice does not prove meanings.
Do not treat the adjacent mnevent.c and mnsoundtest.c edits as decomp lessons beyond formatting/include-order evidence.
Do not claim human reviewer intent or approval rationale; no loaded human review comments are present.
